gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/225)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/225)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) رَجُلٌ يُعَيَّنُ ثُمَّ حَلَّ دَيْنُهُ فَلَمْ يَجِدْ مَا يَقْضِي أَ يَتَعَيَّنُ مِنْ صَاحِبِهِ الَّذِي عَيَّنَهُ وَ يَقْضِيهِ قَالَ نَعَمْ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Sayf Bin Umeyra, from Abu Bakr Al Hazramy who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘A man contracted a buy-back deal (يُعَيَّنُ), then his debt was due, but he could not find what he could fulfil it with, can he do (another) buy-back from his companion whom he had contracted the (first) buy-back with, and fulfil it (the first deal)?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ahmad ibn Muhammad has narrated from Ali ibn al-Hakam from Sayf ibn ‘ Amirah from abu Bakr af-Hadramiy who has said the foifowing: “This is concerning my question before abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘A man who is engaged in an al- ’Aynah deal and the payment date has become due but he cannot find anything to pay back his debt; if he can arrange another al- ’Aynah with the same creditor again.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, he can do so again.’”
